problem_statement: After a new build, yaptap crashes immediately on launch with NSInvalidArgumentException: '-[NSSystemStatusBar statusItems]: unrecognized selector sent to instance'. The crash occurs in run_app() before the main event loop starts, so the menu bar icon never appears. All Phase 3 functionality is unreachable.

exception.reason: -[NSSystemStatusBar statusItems]: unrecognized selector sent to instance 0x6000013e0640
exception.root_cause: statusItems is not a documented public property of NSStatusBar. The runtime class returned by [NSStatusBar systemStatusBar] is NSSystemStatusBar (a private Apple class). On the current macOS version NSSystemStatusBar does not expose the statusItems selector — it was either never public or was removed. The code at app.rs:530-545 (P3-T034 implementation) unconditionally calls this selector to find the most recently created NSStatusItem and mark its image as a template image. There is no nil-guard or @try wrapper, so the unrecognised selector terminates the process.

note: Revision 2 (2026-04-10): P5-I003 (build verification) removed as a standalone task — its checks are absorbed into P5-I001's definition of done. P5-I005 (dark mode icon check) removed — merged into P5-I004 as the final verification step. P5-I002 stripped of private-API alternatives (_statusItems, NSTimer enumeration) that repeat the same problem as the crash; DOD is now binary. P5-I010 scoped to diagnostic checks only — Input Monitoring runtime detection via synthetic key events is a new feature, out of scope for this bug fix phase. 'done' means confirmed correct by code review or test; 'to do' means action required.

issues.title: Remove the statusItems unsafe block that crashes on startup (src/app.rs:530-545)
issues.description: The unsafe block at app.rs:530-545 calls `msg_send![status_bar, statusItems]` on the object returned by `[NSStatusBar systemStatusBar]`. The runtime class is NSSystemStatusBar, which does not recognise the statusItems selector on the current macOS version, producing a fatal NSInvalidArgumentException before any UI is shown. This block was the P3-T034 implementation for marking the tray icon as a macOS template image. The entire block must be deleted — the template image question is addressed separately in P5-I002.
checks: Delete the unsafe block at app.rs lines 530-545 in its entirety.
Remove any `use cocoa::base::{nil, YES}` imports that were only referenced within that block and are now unused.
Run `cargo build` and confirm exit 0.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and confirm no warnings (per rust-style.mdc).
Run `cargo test` and confirm all hotkey unit tests still pass.

issues.title: Set the tray icon as a macOS template image without using private API
issues.description: The spec (menubar.md) requires the icon to be a macOS template image so the OS renders it correctly in both light and dark menu bar appearances. After removing the crashed implementation (P5-I001), no template flag is set. The icons are black-on-transparent, which works in light mode but will be invisible on a dark menu bar. The fix must use only public API — no private selectors.
checks: Inspect the tray-icon 0.14 public API for a template image option: run `cargo doc --open` for tray-icon and search for 'template'. If TrayIconBuilder exposes `with_template_image()` or equivalent, use it and mark this issue done.
If no API exists in 0.14, check whether upgrading to the latest tray-icon patch or minor release adds template support: run `cargo update tray-icon --precise <latest>` and re-inspect the docs.

If no tray-icon API exists in any available version, add a `// FIXME(P5-I002): template image requires tray-icon API not available in current version; icon renders as literal bitmap` comment at the former block location (app.rs:530) and close the task. The icons are black-on-transparent; they will be invisible on a dark menu bar until this is resolved.
After whichever outcome is reached, verify in P5-I004.

issues.title: Verify app starts, menu works, and icon is visible in both light and dark menu bar
issues.description: After fixing P5-I001 and P5-I002, confirm the full startup path works and the icon is legible in both menu bar appearances. This is the combined regression check for the crash fix.
checks: Run `./target/debug/yaptap` with no arguments and confirm no crash.
Confirm the three-bar equalizer icon appears in the menu bar.
Click the icon and confirm the dropdown menu opens with all expected items: prompts list, No Prompt, Hotkey display (disabled), Open Config, Quit YapTap.
Confirm the process does not appear in the Dock: run `lsappinfo list | grep yaptap` and confirm type=UIElement.

Switch macOS to Dark appearance (System Settings → Appearance → Dark) and confirm the icon is visible. If it is invisible (black on black), the template image flag was not set and P5-I002 must be re-opened.
Switch back to Light appearance and confirm the icon is visible.

issues.title: transcription.rs and llm.rs reference scripts by process-cwd-relative path — fail in app mode (inherited from P4-I013)
issues.description: src/transcription.rs passes `"src/core/transcribe.py"` and src/llm.rs passes `"src/core/llm.py"` to python3 as relative paths. These resolve against the process working directory. In app mode (launched from Finder, login items, or a different terminal directory), the cwd is not the project root and python3 reports 'can't open file'. Every hotkey cycle in app mode fails at the pipeline step with a PipelineDone(Err) alert. The crash fix (P5-I001) unblocks reaching this code path, making this the next functional blocker.
checks: After fixing P5-I001, press the hotkey twice in app mode. Confirm the error alert says 'can't open file src/core/transcribe.py' (reproducing the bug).

Fix: resolve the script path relative to the binary, mirroring the prompts_dir() pattern in config.rs. In transcription.rs, replace the literal `"src/core/transcribe.py"` with `std::env::current_exe().ok().and_then(|p| p.parent().map(|d| d.join("../../src/core/transcribe.py"))).unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("src/core/transcribe.py"))`. Apply the same fix to llm.rs for `"src/core/llm.py"`.
Confirm from a different cwd: run `cd /tmp && /path/to/target/debug/yaptap`, press the hotkey twice, and confirm transcription succeeds.

issues.title: Right Option key maps to rdev::Key::AltGr, not Key::Alt — right ⌥Option never triggers the hotkey (inherited from P4-I008)
issues.description: parse_modifier() in hotkey.rs returns rdev::Key::Alt for the 'option' token (left Option, hardware keycode 58). The right Option key (keycode 61) maps to rdev::Key::AltGr in rdev's macOS backend. The modifier check at app.rs uses `modifiers.iter().all(|m| pressed.contains(m))`, which only tests for Alt. Right Option is never counted as satisfying the 'option' modifier, so users who habitually use the right Option key cannot trigger recording.
checks: Test: with the default 'option+space' hotkey, press RIGHT ⌥Option + Space. Confirm the icon does not switch to active.
Test: press LEFT ⌥Option + Space. Confirm it does switch to active.

Fix: in the `rdev::EventType::KeyPress(key)` handler in app.rs, change the modifier check to treat Alt and AltGr as interchangeable for the 'option' modifier: replace `parsed.modifiers.iter().all(|m| pressed.contains(m))` with a check that also counts AltGr as satisfying an Alt requirement. The simplest implementation: before the all() check, if pressed.contains(&rdev::Key::AltGr), also insert rdev::Key::Alt into a local copy of the pressed set.

issues.title: Key auto-repeat fires multiple HotkeyPressed events per physical press — recording stops before release (inherited from P4-I009)
issues.description: macOS sends repeated KeyPress events (~10–30 Hz) while a key is held. The rdev callback at app.rs fires AppEvent::HotkeyPressed on every KeyPress where the full combo is satisfied. Holding ⌥Space for more than ~100 ms sends multiple HotkeyPressed events before the key is released: the first transitions Idle→Recording, and the next transitions Recording→Processing with zero audio captured.
checks: Hold ⌥Space for ~300 ms. Confirm the app transitions directly to Processing (active icon returns to idle) without the user releasing the key.

Fix: add a `main_key_down: bool` flag in the rdev closure, initialised to false. In the KeyPress arm, fire HotkeyPressed only when `!main_key_down && key == parsed.key`, then set main_key_down = true. In the KeyRelease arm, reset main_key_down = false when key == parsed.key. This suppresses auto-repeat without changing any other behavior.

issues.title: Hotkey fires when modifier is pressed after main key is already held — accidental recording start (inherited from P4-I011)
issues.description: The rdev callback fires HotkeyPressed whenever the pressed-keys set satisfies the full combo, regardless of which key was just pressed. If the user holds Space then presses Option, the combo is momentarily satisfied and recording starts unexpectedly. This can trigger accidental recordings during normal typing in other apps.
checks: With another app focused, hold Space then press Option. Confirm yaptap starts recording unexpectedly.

Fix: inside the `rdev::EventType::KeyPress(key)` arm, change the fire condition from `if modifiers_held && pressed.contains(&parsed.key)` to `if modifiers_held && key == parsed.key`. The `key` variable is already bound in the match arm. This is a one-character change that restricts firing to the leading edge of the main key only.

issues.title: Accessibility and Input Monitoring permissions must both be granted for the hotkey to work — diagnostic checklist (inherited from P4-I001, P4-I002)
issues.description: rdev requires Accessibility permission (checked via AXIsProcessTrusted() at launch) and, on macOS 14+, also Input Monitoring permission. The app shows an alert if Accessibility is absent, but does not check Input Monitoring. On macOS 14+, if Input Monitoring is absent, rdev::listen() starts without error but the OS silently delivers no keyboard events — the hotkey is non-functional with no user feedback.
checks: Run `sw_vers -productVersion`. If >= 14.0, open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Input Monitoring and confirm yaptap is listed and its checkbox is checked. If absent, add the binary manually and re-launch.
Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility and confirm yaptap is listed and checked. Note: debug and release builds have different binary paths and require separate permission entries.
After granting either permission, quit and re-launch. Permissions do not take effect until the process restarts.

Confirm the rdev thread started: with RUST_LOG=debug, launch yaptap and check Console.app for the 'rdev thread spawned' log line (add this tracing::debug! call to app.rs at the thread::spawn site if not yet present).

issues.title: eprintln! used for all config diagnostics in config.rs — violates rust-tracing.mdc (inherited from P4-I022)
issues.description: AppConfig::load() and save_prompt() use eprintln! for all warnings (failed directory creation, TOML parse errors, invalid hotkey, failed saves). rust-tracing.mdc rule: 'Never use println! or eprintln! for observability.' In app mode stderr is invisible; these warnings are silently lost. Note: P5-I012 separately handles the user-facing show_alert() requirement for parse and hotkey errors — this task covers only the tracing conversion.
checks: Run `grep -n 'eprintln!' src/config.rs` — expect ~12 matches.

Replace each eprintln! with the appropriate tracing level: I/O and serialisation errors → `tracing::warn!`; use structured key-value fields per rust-tracing.mdc: `tracing::warn!(path = %path.display(), error = %e, "could not read config file")`.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` to confirm no new warnings.
Run `RUST_LOG=warn ./target/debug/yaptap` and confirm config warnings appear in the terminal output.

issues.title: Config parse and hotkey errors never show a UI alert in app mode — violates spec config.md (inherited from P4-I014)
issues.description: config.md specifies: TOML parse error → alert 'Config file is not valid TOML: ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml — using defaults.'; invalid hotkey → alert 'Unknown hotkey "<value>" — using default: option+space.' AppConfig::load() is called before finishLaunching (app.rs:429), so show_alert() cannot be called from within load(). The result is that users with a corrupt config.toml see no alert and have no indication why defaults were applied. Note: P5-I011 separately adds tracing::warn! for internal observability — this task adds the required user-visible dialogs.
checks: Introduce a TOML syntax error in ~/.config/yaptap/config.toml (e.g. add a bare `[[[`) and launch the app. Confirm no alert appears (reproducing the bug).

Fix: change AppConfig::load() to return (AppConfig, Vec<String>) where the Vec carries deferred human-readable warning messages. In run_app(), after the finishLaunching call (app.rs:426), iterate the warnings and call show_alert() for each before continuing startup.
Re-test with corrupt config — confirm the alert 'Config file is not valid TOML...' appears after the menu bar icon is visible.
Re-test with an invalid hotkey value (e.g. hotkey = "win+space") — confirm the alert 'Unknown hotkey...' appears.

issues.title: unsafe blocks in app.rs lack // SAFETY: comments — violates rust-safety.mdc (inherited from P4-I023)
issues.description: rust-safety.mdc rule: 'Every unsafe block must have a comment explaining the invariant that makes it sound.' app.rs contains multiple unsafe blocks with no SAFETY justification: show_alert() NSAlert/NSString FFI, run_app() NSApplication setup, the event loop nextEventMatchingMask/sendEvent/updateWindows block, and the autorelease pool drain.
checks: Run `grep -n 'unsafe' src/app.rs` to list all sites.

Add a `// SAFETY:` comment above each unsafe block explaining: (a) Cocoa object pointers are valid because they come from documented AppKit factory methods that never return null for these calls, (b) all msg_send! calls execute on the main thread, (c) the autorelease pool drain is sound because the pool was created on this same thread in this same loop iteration.

issues.title: #[allow(clippy::comparison_to_empty)] suppresses a fixable lint — violates rust-style.mdc (inherited from P4-I024)
issues.description: rust-style.mdc rule: 'Run clippy with cargo clippy -- -D warnings; every warning is a bug.' app.rs uses `config.selected_prompt == ""` at two sites, suppressed with #[allow(clippy::comparison_to_empty)]. The idiomatic fix is `.is_empty()`, which removes the need for the allow attribute.
checks: Replace both occurrences of `config.selected_prompt == ""` with `config.selected_prompt.is_empty()`.
Remove the two corresponding `#[allow(clippy::comparison_to_empty)]` attributes.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and confirm clean.

issues.title: let _ = menu.append() silently discards errors in build_menu() — violates rust-errors.mdc (inherited from P4-I025)
issues.description: rust-errors.mdc rule: 'BAD — silently discards errors: `let _ = do_thing();`'. build_menu() uses `let _ = menu.append(...)` for every menu item. If tray-icon returns an error, the item is silently omitted from the menu with no log.
checks: Run `grep -n 'let _ = menu.append' src/app.rs` — expect ~9 matches.
Replace each with `if let Err(e) = menu.append(...) { tracing::warn!(error = ?e, "failed to append menu item"); }` per rust-tracing.mdc structured field convention.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and confirm clean.
